pattern: A number has exactly three divisors precisely when it is the square of a prime.
hints: Count the divisors of n directly, or reason about which numbers can have exactly three.
Three divisors means 1, the number itself, and exactly one more in between.
That middle divisor must be a prime p with n = p*p, so check whether n is a perfect square of a prime.

edge_inputs.description: square of a prime has three divisors
perfect square but not of a prime
largest perfect square in range that is a prime square

stress.description: scan many values across the full range
